title: centratherum camporum var. albicans
intro: Centratherum camporum var. albicans, also known as the white centratherum, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Centratherum camporum var. albicans include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids in its leaves and flowers. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and skin infections, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria and viruses, and protecting against oxidative stress. Centratherum camporum var. albicans is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping its leaves and flowers in hot water, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
